mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling, the discount rate on equities declines and financial conditions ease, so S&P 500 prices should rise over the next month. This signal is falsified if rate-cut periods do not produce positive 20-day forward returns relative to non-cut periods.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of windows where FEDFUNDS declines over the prior month, or the average return during those windows is below the unconditional mean, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in RSAFS above its recent trend signals stronger household and business spending, which should support earnings expectations and lift S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect fails when sales acceleration does not translate into positive 20-day equity returns.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with RSAFS above its 6-month trend do not show higher 20-day SPX returns than periods below trend, or if the sign of the return differential is negative.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in UNRATE above a recent baseline indicates labor-market deterioration, which should weaken earnings expectations and pressure S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is invalid if rising unemployment is not followed by negative 20-day equity returns.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the mean triggered return exceeds zero.

mechanism_narrative: A joint improvement in PAYEMS and a decline in UNRATE identifies broad labor-market tightening, which should raise confidence in future consumption and earnings and push S&P 500 returns higher over the next month. The claim fails if the combined labor-strength pattern does not outperform either series alone.
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE does not produce higher 20-day SPX returns than either a PAYEMS-only or UNRATE-only signal, or if the combined signal has no positive average return.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO reflects weaker industrial output and slower real activity, which should reduce cyclical earnings expectations and lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism fails when falling production is not followed by equity weakness.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not lower when INDPRO is trending down versus when it is flat or rising, or if the sign of the conditional average return is positive.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST signals weakening residential demand and construction activity, which should precede softer growth expectations and negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal is falsified if housing starts contractions are not followed by weaker 60-day equity performance.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day SPX returns after HOUST declines greater than 5% are not below the unconditional mean, or if triggered periods show positive average returns.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation above trend combined with a restrictive policy rate creates a higher real-rate shock that compresses equity valuations, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next month. The claim fails when high CPI and high FEDFUNDS do not jointly coincide with lower returns than either condition alone.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of elevated CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS does not produce lower 20-day SPX returns than either elevated inflation alone or elevated policy rates alone, or if the joint average return is non-negative.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized upturn in GDPC1 and RSAFS indicates broad real-demand strength that should support corporate revenues and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The mechanism is falsified if combined growth in output and sales does not lead to superior 60-day equity performance.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with simultaneous GDPC1 and RSAFS acceleration do not show higher 60-day SPX returns than periods where only one of the two accelerates, or if the combined signal has a negative mean return.
